method_atom: A framework for analysing the joint correlation structure of a two-party, many-variable system through the second-moment (covariance) matrix of a multivariate Gaussian distribution, and using it to decide whether the joint distribution factorises into a convex mixture of independent product distributions over the two parties. A necessary positivity criterion based on a sign-flip / transpose-type operation applied to one party's variables is tested for sufficiency, and a low-dimensional parametric family of explicit counterexamples shows that this necessary criterion fails to guarantee factorisability once each party carries at least two coupled variables.
object_atom: Bipartite quantum states of harmonic oscillators (continuous-variable bosonic modes) with Gaussian Wigner functions shared between two parties, Alice and Bob — in particular bound-entangled Gaussian states with two oscillators on each side, where positivity of the partial transpose no longer implies separability.
brief_reason: The covariance-matrix test of Gaussian factorisability and the necessary-vs-sufficient partial-transpose criterion carry to any Gaussian correlation problem, while the harmonic-oscillator continuous-variable quantum states and their entanglement are the physics-specific object.
